summary: Comprehensive backend test suite for Predictive Compliance Vault (PCV) — all 5 sub-systems covered (BackgroundIntegrityDaemon, RegulatoryOracleNetwork, SmartRemediationAgent, PortfolioIntegrityGraph, EvidencePacketBuilder). 41/41 tests passed in 7.3s. Public verifier confirmed to work without Authorization header and stored_hash == recomputed_hash byte-for-byte.

minor.issue: Returns 200 with {ok:false} on nonexistent anchor instead of 404. Inconsistent with other PCV not-found endpoints (e.g. /remediation/tasks/{id} which raises 404). Consider returning 404 for consistency.

action_items: (Optional) Make POST /portfolio/graph/{anchor_id}/anchor-hedera raise HTTPException(404) on missing anchor instead of returning {ok:false} body for HTTP-consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: pcv_service.py is 909 lines — file size exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into 5 modules matching the 5 sub-systems (integrity_daemon.py, regulatory_oracle.py, remediation_agent.py, portfolio_graph.py, evidence_packet.py) plus a shared utils module.

pcv_routes.py line 167-174: list_scores constructs Mongo query inline rather than going through pcv_service. Other endpoints delegate to the service layer — inconsistent abstraction. Move to pcv_service.list_compliance_scores().
pcv_routes.py line 67-71: list_integrity_scans also queries db directly — same inconsistency, should delegate to service.

pcv_service.score_portfolio_against_rules (line 414-428): uses $set with a fresh uuid.uuid4().hex on every upsert call. On re-scoring, the score id changes even if the doc already existed — should use $setOnInsert for the id field or stable id (e.g. notarization_id) to keep stable identity.

pcv_service._record_integrity_issue lacks 'auto_resolve match' — on re-scan, if same notarization with same hash mismatch persists, a duplicate issue row is inserted each scan. Consider deduping by (notarization_id, kind, recorded_hash, current_hash) before insert.

Hedera fallback (line 697 & 801) generates simulated tx_id but never marks the row as simulated=True. Court-readiness suggests adding a boolean 'simulated' flag so verifiers/auditors can distinguish real Hedera anchors from fallbacks.
pcv_service.set_dependencies stores hedera_service & email_service as module-level globals. acceptable for MVP but consider DI container for testability.
seed_data_creation: Tests use existing portfolio. Idempotent regulatory seed (7 rules) confirmed: rerunning POST /api/pcv/regulatory/seed returns inserted_or_updated=0. One TEST_ regulatory change event recorded, one TEST_ evidence packet generated, remediation tasks approved+completed. All test artifacts prefixed TEST_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PCV is fully functional. 41 pytest cases cover happy paths + 422 Pydantic validation + 404 not-found + idempotency + public verifier (no auth) + hash byte-for-byte match. The only nit is the anchor-hedera not-found path returns 200+{ok:false} instead of 404 — flagged but not blocking. Hedera SDK in this env throws on submit_message, so simulated tx_ids in format '0.0.10373605@ts.nonce' are observed (expected per problem statement).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found, only minor design nits.
